_doc: Project config — the one home for a project's choices, so the core depends on NO specific agent. The Orchestrator resolves a seat through `roles` before spawning and reads `mode` for decision authority. A swapped-in agent is bound by its role CONTRACT (PROTOCOL.md `## Role contracts`), not by being ours — the ship-gate checks the verdict's form, not its author. This file is the single home for what used to be a separate decision-authority marker, the project-kind marker, and an implicit role wiring. Absent file or unrecognised value ⇒ the safe defaults below (mode: interactive).
_mode: autonomous | interactive — value-home for PROTOCOL.md invariant 7. Absent or unrecognised ⇒ interactive (the safe default; never an error).
_profile: full | lite | solo — the speed↔trust tradeoff (PROTOCOL.md `## Project config`). Absent or unrecognised ⇒ full (the safe default; zero-config = full rigor, never an error). States only the WISH for the CUTTABLE levers (who builds, plan ceremony). The FLOOR — independent review by a separate fresh Reviewer, the honesty gates, the merge stamp, and the Operator merges — holds in EVERY profile, enforced regardless of this value. A profile that cuts the floor is not a faster protocol; it is no protocol. - full: orchestrator routes, spawns a Builder; full plan + Operator approval (today). - lite: orchestrator MAY build directly; plan ceremony may be trimmed (fixup-grade). - solo: lite + plan ceremony off by default. Same floor.
_platform: claude | opencode — which adapter realises the contract on this project. Usually auto-detected; pinned here when needed.
_kind: software | documentation — seeds the quality-layer defaults and the review route (a doc-kind project validates rather than code-reviews).
_roles: Each seat binds an `agent` and an optional `model`. `agent` = the id the active platform knows (a Claude subagent_type / an OpenCode agent id); swap it for ANY agent that honours the seat's contract — that is how you plug in a different reviewer (stricter, or an external engine) with zero core edit. `model` states only the WISH ('session' / 'auto' / a per-platform pin); the platform adapter realises it. Omitted ⇒ 'session', EXCEPT the reviewer defaults to 'auto' (a maker-model can't see its own blind spots). The model policy — what 'auto' resolves to and each platform's model authority — is homed in the platform adapter: adapter/tool-map.json `models`.
